components.name: AppShell
components.purpose: Top-level four-pane layout (strip / sidebar / main / spine). Each of the sidebar and spine is independently togglable; when collapsed, a narrow pane-rail renders a single reveal IconButton. Keyboard: ⌘\ strip, ⌘[ sidebar, ⌘] spine. Carries a data-tab-style attribute (A / B / C) driven by the SurfaceDevPanel for live A/B of the tab register.

components.name: ProjectStrip
components.purpose: Vertical icon nav across projects. Top: project squares (with a pulsing activity dot when any workspace in that project is active or needs attention). Middle: transparent IconButton for New project. Bottom cluster: Settings + Help (each opens the AppDialog). No quick-switcher icon — ⌘K is the only entry point.

components.name: WorkspaceSidebar
components.purpose: Sits directly on the page surface (no fill, no border) per the two-tier register. Project title (self-describing name + path) + collapse IconButton + Home button + Workspaces list (status icon + name, single line — branch name lives in the row's title attribute for hover). Via WorkspaceStatusIcon, the 7-variant status set reads the same here as on HomeTabA. Pane resizer sits at the floating-surface edge (cursor-only feedback; no visible bar). 13.E: when the active workspace has at least one mergeable track, a Request Merge IconButton (GitPullRequest) renders inline next to the project title — runs cmd_request_merge on the most recent eligible track.

components.name: MainView
components.purpose: Routes between project-home and workspace view. Wraps the tab body (or home surface) in a .main-surface rectangle — the only Tier-2 raised surface on the page. Tabs bar sits above the surface on the page; the active tab merges into the surface top edge (styles A + B) or floats separately (style C) based on the [data-tab-style] attribute carried by .app-shell. Project-home topbar occupies the same vertical slot as the tabs-bar and carries only the Panels/Palette variant toggle.

components.name: TabLayout
components.purpose: Tab body primitive: scrollable content region + optional bottom dock slot for compose-style inputs. Matched max-width between scroll and dock so the dock feels aligned with content, not anchored to window edges.

components.name: ActivitySpine
components.purpose: Sits directly on the page surface (no fill, no border) per the two-tier register. Right-rail hierarchical status column, scoped to the active project (narrows further to the active workspace when one is selected). Events are filtered to streams that belong to the scope. Summary renders as dot+count per non-zero state; zero-state fallback is 'Nothing streaming'. Rows are text-only with per-depth repeating-linear-gradient indent rails. Header carries a collapse IconButton (⌘]) and a hover-revealed .pane-toggle on the left edge.

components.name: QuickSwitcher
components.purpose: Cmd+K fuzzy search across projects and workspaces. Dialog with focus trap.

components.name: HomeTabA
components.purpose: Project home — single-column dashboard. Sections in order: Needs your attention (only when non-empty), Near-term focus, Active workspaces (WorkspaceStatusIcon + name + one-line summary from the first open tab), Recent reports, Autonomy (interactive SegmentedToggle with optimistic local update via setAutonomyOverride — Phase 13 will swap in IPC). No kicker. Panels, not cards; hairline dividers between groups. The Home A/B toggle was pruned 2026-04-22 — Panels is committed.

components.name: HomeTabB
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-23. The palette variant of project home was consolidated into Variant A (Panels). File deleted; entry retained for history.

components.name: WorkspaceThread
components.purpose: The single tab body for every workspace tab post-Phase 13.1 (spec Decision 36). One continuous scrollable thread of typed artifact blocks (resolved via the block registry) plus a docked ComposeDock. Per-tab `hasStarted` state — fresh tabs render context-aware suggestions sourced from the workspace's recent artifacts; first send flips to thread mode. Subscribes to `artifact_*` stream events filtered to the production wire format `workspace:<uuid>` (also tolerates the bare-uuid mock format) for live refresh. Phase 13.D wired `onSend` to `ipcClient().postMessage` with synchronous `useRef` re-entry guard against double-dispatch, draft restoration via the ComposeDock imperative handle on failure, and typed `IpcError` translation via `ipc/error.ts` (cost-cap-exceeded / scope-denied / etc. each get their own copy).

components.name: PlanTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Plan/Design/Build/Blank tab types collapsed into a single WorkspaceThread surface. File deleted.

components.name: DesignTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). File deleted.

components.name: BuildTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Chat + slash-commands behavior absorbed into WorkspaceThread + ComposeDock. File deleted.

components.name: BlankTab
components.purpose: Retired 2026-04-24 (spec Decision 36). Empty-state behavior is now the suggestions view inside WorkspaceThread. File deleted.

components.name: BlockRenderers
components.purpose: Twelve typed-artifact renderers registered against the block registry (`packages/app/src/blocks/registry.ts`) on import of `packages/app/src/blocks/index.ts`. Real-data renderers: Message, Spec, CodeChange, Pr, Approval, Comment, TaskList, Prototype (Phase 13.F: renders inline-HTML payloads via `PrototypePreview` with `sandbox=""` + injected CSP). Stub renderers (title + summary) still pending: Report, Diagram, Variant, TrackRollup. GenericBlock is the fallback for unknown kinds. ApprovalBlock (Phase 13.G) parses the inline JSON payload for `approval_id`, calls `cmd_resolve_approval` on Grant/Deny with optimistic flip + projector truth via `approval_granted/denied` stream subscription. Buttons disabled when payload lacks a parsable id. Every block is an <article> with BlockHeader (kind badge, title, author, expand/pin actions). All visual decisions route through tokens — no inline styles.

components.name: ComposeDock
components.purpose: Shared compose surface for WorkspaceThread (and any future tab variants). Owns its own draft, attachments, model/effort/plan-mode controls, and drag-and-drop target. Imperative handle (`setDraft`, `focus`) lets the parent seed prompts and restore drafts on send failure (used by WorkspaceThread.onSend's catch handler — the dock clears its draft synchronously after `onSend` returns, so failure recovery has to re-seed via the handle). 13.D wired `onSend` through to `Orchestrator::post_message` via `cmd_post_message`; the parent is responsible for in-flight gating and error display.

components.name: SettingsPage
components.purpose: Full-page Settings surface (replaces the modal that previously lived in AppDialog). Left rail of section anchors (Appearance, Account, Models, Preferences); content pane with section headers and rows. Mounted from App.tsx when `dialog === 'settings'` and takes over the whole shell viewport. Esc closes; AppDialog now renders Help only. 13.E: Account section now carries a Repository row — shows the linked path (or 'not linked') and a Link / Re-link button that opens RepoLinkModal scoped to the active workspace.

components.name: SurfaceDevPanel
components.purpose: Dev-only floating panel (⌘.) that exposes seven knobs over the surface register: Compose fill, Main tab fill, Surface sand, Tab opacity, Border intensity, Shadow intensity, plus a Tab-corners variant toggle (Soft/Concentric/Folder/Match/Custom) backed by a 0-32px slider, and Main tab + Compose radius sliders. Persists to localStorage. Only mounted when `import.meta.env.MODE === 'development'`; the production build never ships it.

components.name: StreamingText
components.purpose: Claude-style character-by-character text reveal with blink cursor. Reduced-motion falls back to instant.

components.name: Tooltip
components.purpose: Custom-popover tooltip that replaces the HTML title attribute. Appears immediately on hover and on keyboard focus, renders in a fixed-position layer anchored via getBoundingClientRect (no overflow clipping), supports a separate shortcut slot rendered as right-aligned kbd, announces via aria-describedby. 'auto' placement flips to top or bottom based on available room. Reduced-motion skips the entrance animation.

components.name: IconButton
components.purpose: Single icon-only button archetype. Two sizes (sm 24×24 for dense inline affordances, md 32×32 for navigation/topbar/compose). Required tooltip label (wraps children in Tooltip), optional shortcut, optional pressed state, transparent by default with a btn-icon--primary modifier for the filled Send variant. Focus ring on the outer button so the entire hit target receives focus.

components.name: SegmentedToggle
components.purpose: Two-to-N pill toggle. Collapses the identical Panels/Palette variant toggle (MainView) and Bounded/Open density toggle (Palette) into one component. Each option takes a value, label, optional tooltip, and optional icon. CSS lives under `.segmented-toggle` / `.segmented-toggle__btn`.

components.name: Icons
components.purpose: Thin wrappers around lucide-react for the canonical set reused 3+ times across the app: IconX, IconPlus, IconBranch, IconChevronLeft/Right, IconCollapseLeft/Right. Defaults size and strokeWidth per axiom #13 (12/14/16 px, 1.25 stroke at sm/md, 1.5 at lg). One-off icons are imported from lucide-react directly in the consumer file, passing size/strokeWidth inline.

components.name: WorkspaceStatusIcon
components.purpose: Single source of truth for the 7-variant workspace status glyph. Used by WorkspaceSidebar rows and HomeTabA's Active-workspaces section so status reads identically everywhere. Each status maps to a Lucide icon and a semantic-color CSS variable on .workspace-status.

components.name: Palette
components.purpose: Shared palette primitive — prompt + 4–6 context-aware suggestions. Default consumer is BlankTab (workspace-scoped). Default density is 'open' — bare input on the surface with a blinking caret, no container; suggestions render directly on the surface. 'bounded' density stays as a runtime alternative for surfaces that want prompt + suggestions in a single rounded object.

components.name: PaneResizer
components.purpose: Edge drag handle for the workspace sidebar and activity spine. Pointer-captured drag writes in-memory via `onLiveChange` (no localStorage during drag); pointer-up calls `onCommit` to flush the latest width to disk. Double-click resets to default and commits. Arrow keys ±16px (±48px with shift) commit immediately. Width clamps to [180, 480]. `role="separator"` + `aria-valuemin/max/now`.

components.name: AppDialog
components.purpose: Modal for Settings and Help. Settings carries a live Theme picker (System / Light / Dark SegmentedToggle) plus placeholder rows for Density / Account / Models / Preferences. Help shows a question input, a keyboard-shortcut dl, and an About row. Inner row dividers use --color-border-soft so they don't double up against the outer dialog frame. Scrim click and Escape close. Triggered from the ProjectStrip bottom cluster (Settings + Help IconButtons) and from the ⌘? shortcut.

components.name: Onboarding
components.purpose: Three-slide first-run introduction. The final slide's primary action launches RepoLinkModal when a workspace already exists; otherwise it falls back to a plain 'Get started'. Dismissible; persists in localStorage.

components.name: RepoLinkModal
components.purpose: Phase 13.E. Action-attached form modal that collects an absolute path to a git work-tree, validates it via cmd_link_repo (canonicalizes + validate_repo), and persists the link on the active workspace. Surfaces from the Onboarding final slide and from Settings → Account. Reuses the existing app-dialog__* token-driven CSS — no new tokens. A11y: role=dialog + aria-modal, aria-labelledby, focus on input on open, Escape dismiss, Tab/Shift-Tab focus trap, scrim dismiss via onClick (not onMouseDown so a drag inside the dialog ending on the scrim doesn't surprise-dismiss). Errors render through role=alert and aria-describedby on the input.

components.name: ComponentCatalog
components.purpose: Live tile grid showing each Mini-inspired component and its token binding, for the Design tab.

components.name: PrototypePreview
components.purpose: Sandboxed iframe rendering agent-produced prototype HTML. Two prop forms: { workspace } drives the lab demo (variant explorer + annotation layer); { inlineHtml, title? } is the artifact-thread form used by PrototypeBlock — bare iframe with sandbox="" and CSP <meta> injected for defense-in-depth.

components.name: AnnotationLayer
components.purpose: Pin-drop overlay on top of the prototype iframe. Click empty canvas to drop a pin; type a note; save. Every saved pin is a numbered button that opens a popover with the note, author, and a Remove action. Draft popover supports Enter-to-save, Escape-to-cancel, explicit Cancel button.

components.name: VariantExplorer
components.purpose: Variant selector for the prototype preview.

components.name: CostChip
components.purpose: Phase 13.G workspace topbar widget. Shows `<spent> / <cap>` with a colored band (50% green / 80% amber / >80% red, dimmed when no cap). Click expands a popover with daily / weekly / per-track breakdown (week + per-track placeholders defer to a follow-up). Hidden by default per spec Decision 34; the COST_CHIP_PREFERENCE_EVENT custom event triggers a re-fetch when Settings flips the toggle. Subscribes to the `cost_recorded` stream event so the chip updates per turn without explicit refresh.

components.name: KeychainStatusReadout
components.purpose: Phase 13.G Settings → Account row. Polls `cmd_get_keychain_status` once on mount and renders a stable copy + state dot (connected / disconnected / unsupported_os). `aria-live='polite'` so screen readers don't get re-announced on minor state churn. Decision 26 — the readout never reads or writes the secret bytes, only confirms the credential's presence in the macOS Keychain.

components.name: CostChipToggle
components.purpose: Phase 13.G Settings → Preferences row. Reuses SegmentedToggle for an Off / On chooser bound to `cmd_set_cost_chip_preference` (persists `cost_chip_enabled` in `~/.designer/settings.json`). Dispatches the `COST_CHIP_PREFERENCE_EVENT` custom event after writing so the topbar `CostChip` re-renders without a full reload.
